author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice
book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice.
The Canons of the CCXVII Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage. Commonly Called The Code of Canons of the African Church.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice

title: Canon XVIII.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Canon XVIII.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: The Latin caption is the canon of the Greek.)

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: If any cleric is ordained he ought to be admonished to observe the constitutions.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: And that neither the Eucharist nor Baptism should be given to the bodies of the dead.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: And that every year in every province the Metropolitans come together in synod.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It seemed good that before bishops, or clerics were ordained, the provisions of the canons should be brought to their notice, lest, they might afterwards repent of having through ignorance acted contrary to law.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Ancient Epitome of Greek Canon XIX.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: The things which have been adopted by the synods should be made known to him who is to be ordained.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It also seemed good that the Eucharist should not be given to the bodies of the dead.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: For it is written: “Take, Eat,” but the bodies of the dead can neither “take” nor “eat.”

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Nor let the ignorance of the presbyters baptize those who are dead.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Ancient Epitome of Greek Canon XX

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: The Eucharist is not to be given to the body of one dead for it neither eats nor drinks.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: The ignorance of a presbyter shall not baptize a dead man.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: And therefore in this holy synod should be confirmed in accordance with the Nicene decrees, on account of Ecclesiastical causes, which often are delayed to the injury of the people, that every year there should be a synod, to which all, who are primates of the provinces, should send bishops as legates, from their own synods, two or as many as they choose; so that when the synod meets it may have full power to act.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Ancient Epitome of Greek Canon XXI.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: According to the decrees of the Nicene Fathers a yearly synod shall be assembled, and two legates or as many as they shall choose, shall be sent by the primates of every province.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: This is composed of Canons II., IV., and V. of the second series of enactments of Hippo, a.d.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: The 18th canon in the Edition of Tilius and Bishop Beveridge runs thus; viz. [If any clergyman be ordained he ought to be reminded to keep the canons; and that the Eucharist or Baptism be not given to dead corpses; and that the Metropolitans in every province meet in synod yearly.]

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: They speak their own language, and call him a Metropolitan, whom the Africans called a Primate; but then they have also the entire 18th canon, as it here stands according to the Latin, which they divide into three, and number them 19, 20, 21.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It seems very odd that they should allege the authority of the Nicene Synod upon this occasion; for that orders a synod twice a year, this but once; that intends a provincial synod, this a diocesan or national one.
